overview: Chiranjeevi Reddy Institute of Engineering and Technology (CRIT), established in 2008, is a prominent private institution located in Anantapur, Andhra Pradesh. While the group operates specific wings focused on women's empowerment in technical education, the main campus is known for providing a wide array of undergraduate and postgraduate programs in engineering and management fields.
accreditation: Approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), New Delhi. Affiliated with Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Anantapur (JNTUA).

coursesAndFees.course: B.Tech (CSE, AI&ML, AI&DS, ECE, EEE, Mechanical, Civil)
coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 45% aggregate in PCM + AP EAPCET rank

coursesAndFees.course: M.Tech (Software Engineering, Structural Engineering, DSCE, EPS)

admissions.admissionProcess: Admissions are conducted through state-level counseling (APSCHE). 70% of seats are filled under the Convener Quota (Category A) based on entrance exam ranks. The remaining 30% are Management Quota (Category B) seats filled based on merit or qualifying exam scores.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026: May 2026; AP ICET 2026: May 2026; AP PGECET 2026: May-June 2026.

facilities: Separate Hostels for Boys and Girls
campus.specialNote: The campus is situated on Bellary Road and is recognized for its disciplined environment. It emphasizes a 'complete learning environment' with a high faculty-to-student ratio to ensure personalized academic support.

contact.address: Susheela Nagar, Bellary Road, Anantapur, Andhra Pradesh - 515001
